title: bridge-slack: verify_signature test uses code-under-test as oracle
description: slack/src/slack.rs test verify_signature_accepts_correct_signature() claims 'Oracle: known HMAC computed offline with Python' but the test body recomputes the HMAC in Rust using the same HmacSha256::new_from_slice/mac.update/mac.finalize path as the implementation. This violates DESIGN.md §15 test oracle discipline: the test proves only that the same code computes the same result twice, not that the result is correct. Fix: hardcode a known-good HMAC computed from a fixed timestamp and body using an external tool (Python hmac module, openssl), using a timestamp far enough in the future to not expire (or mock time).
